source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/04_2G & 3G (A1417MCW, A1717MCW, F1417MCW)/▶ CE MDR/230926 기술문서 & 임상평가 보완 리뷰 2 (with 컨설팅)/CE MDR TD 보완_230926/A-TCF-5-162 Vigilance System.docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Procedure defining the company vigilance system for incident reporting, investigation, corrective action, recall, and advisory notices for medical devices.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to guide manufacturer, authorized representative, competent authority, user, recall, and field safety notice activities under medical device vigilance procedures.
keyPoints: The procedure aims to improve protection of health and safety by evaluating reported incidents and disseminating information to prevent recurrence.
Manufacturers or authorized representatives must notify the relevant Competent authority about incidents and field safety corrective actions when reporting criteria are met.
Any event meeting reporting criteria A-C is considered an incident and must be reported to the relevant Competent authority.

Initial incident reporting timelines include not later than 2 calendar days for serious public health threat, 10 elapsed calendar days for death or unanticipated serious deterioration, and 30 elapsed calendar days for others.
Recall and advisory notice procedures include recall committee responsibilities, quarantine and logging of recalled devices, and Field Safety Notice content expectations.

entities.name: President
entities.evidence: Approve Advisory Notice; Approve the contract with Europe Authorized representative; Approve the declaration of conformity

entities.name: Quality management representative (QMR)
entities.evidence: Review Advisory Notice, contract and the declaration of conformity

entities.name: Quality control manager
entities.evidence: Information correction and analysis after producing; Solve the problem described in advisory notice

entities.name: Manufacturer
entities.evidence: The Manufacturer or his Authorized representative shall notify the relevant Competent authority about incidents and field safety corrective actions when the reporting criteria are met.

entities.name: Authorized representative
entities.evidence: The Manufacturer should ensure that these guidelines are made known to their Authorized representatives within the EEA, Switzerland and Turkey

entities.name: Competent authority
entities.evidence: The Competent authority monitors the investigation of the incident carried out by the Manufacturer.

entities.name: Notified Body
entities.evidence: Manufacturers must keep the Notified Body advised of issues occurring in the post production phase affecting the certification.

entities.name: Users
entities.evidence: Users should report incidents with medical devices to the Manufacturer or to the Competent authority depending on national practice.

entities.name: Recall committee
entities.evidence: The recall committee is responsible for initiating the recall and coordinating related activities. The committee includes the President, Quality Assurance Manager and Production Manager.

entities.name: National Competent Authority
entities.evidence: The FIELD SAFETY NOTICE should be written in the language(s) accepted by the National Competent Authority(s).

concepts.name: Vigilance System
concepts.evidence: The Vigilance System is intended to allow data to be correlated between Competent Authorities and Manufacturers and so facilitate corrective action earlier

concepts.name: Incident reporting criteria
concepts.evidence: Any event which meets all three basic reporting criteria A-C listed below is considered as an incident and must be reported to the relevant Competent authority.

concepts.name: Initial incident report
concepts.evidence: QMR must submit an initial incident report to the Competent authority for recording and evaluation.

concepts.name: Serious public health threat reporting timeline
concepts.pageTarget: Serious Public Health Threat Reporting Timeline
concepts.evidence: Serious public health threat: Immediately (without any delay that could not be justified) but not later than 2 calendar days after awareness by the Manufacturer of this threat.

concepts.name: Death or unanticipated serious deterioration reporting timeline
concepts.evidence: Death or Unanticipated serious deterioration in state of health: Immediately ... but not later than 10 elapsed calendar days following the date of awareness of the event.

concepts.name: Other incident reporting timeline
concepts.evidence: Others: Immediately ... but not later than 30 elapsed calendar days following the date of awareness of the event.

concepts.name: Device recall
concepts.evidence: The recall of a device is the removal from the field of a marketed device that is in violation of the related regulations.

concepts.name: Advisory notice
concepts.evidence: An advisory notice is issued to provide information and/or advice on what action should be taken in the use, modification, disposal or return of a medical device.

concepts.name: Field Safety Notice
concepts.evidence: The FIELD SAFETY NOTICE should be written in the language(s) accepted by the National Competent Authority(s).

concepts.name: Market withdrawal and stock recovery
concepts.evidence: Recall does not include market withdrawal or stock recovery.

evidence.claim: The procedure covers actions after the company, authorized representative, or competent authority receives incident information involving a medical device according to Medical Device Regulation.
evidence.evidenceText: This procedure covers the actions to be taken once our company, Authorized representative or competent authority receives information concerning an Incident involving a Medical device according to Medical Device Regulation.

evidence.claim: The purpose is to improve health and safety protection by reducing repeated adverse incidents and disseminating information.
evidence.evidenceText: The purpose of this procedure is to improve the protection of health and safety of patients, users and others by reducing the likelihood of the same type of adverse incident being repeating in different places at different times

evidence.claim: Manufacturers or authorized representatives must notify relevant competent authorities about incidents and field safety corrective actions when reporting criteria are met.
evidence.evidenceText: The Manufacturer or his Authorized representative shall notify the relevant Competent authority about incidents and field safety corrective actions when the reporting criteria are met.

evidence.claim: Manufacturers are responsible for investigating incidents and taking corrective actions.
evidence.evidenceText: The Manufacturer has the responsibility for investigating incidents and for taking any corrective actions necessary.

evidence.claim: Competent authorities monitor manufacturer incident investigations and may take further action.
evidence.evidenceText: The Competent authority monitors the investigation of the incident carried out by the Manufacturer. The Competent authority should take any further action that may be necessary to supplement the actions of the Manufacturer.

evidence.claim: Users should report medical device incidents to the manufacturer or competent authority depending on national practice.
evidence.evidenceText: Users should report incidents with medical devices to the Manufacturer or to the Competent authority depending on national practice.

evidence.claim: An event meeting all three reporting criteria A-C is an incident that must be reported to the relevant Competent authority.
evidence.evidenceText: Any event which meets all three basic reporting criteria A-C listed below is considered as an incident and must be reported to the relevant Competent authority.

evidence.claim: One reporting criterion is that an event has occurred, including testing, supplied information, or scientific information indicating a factor that could lead or has led to an event.
evidence.evidenceText: A: An event has occurred This also includes situations where testing performed on the device, examination of the information supplied with the device or any scientific information indicates some factor that could lead or has led to an event.

evidence.claim: One reporting criterion is that the manufacturer's device is suspected to be a contributory cause of the incident.
evidence.evidenceText: B: The Manufacturer’s device is suspected to be a contributory cause of the incident

evidence.claim: One reporting criterion is that the event led or might have led to death or serious deterioration in health.
evidence.evidenceText: C: The event led, or might have led, to one of the following outcomes: death of a patient, User or other person; serious deterioration in state of health of a patient, User or other person.

evidence.claim: Initial incident reports must include manufacturer and authorized representative contact details, incident knowledge date, device identification, notified body identification, incident details, product status, preliminary comments, corrective action schedule, similar incident information, and EEA sale information.
evidence.evidenceText: The initial report includes following provisions - Name, Authorized Representative’s Name, address, contact number including fax number. - The date when the incident came to the knowledge of the Manufacturer - Medical device kind, commercial name, catalogue number/model, serial/batch/lot number - Identification number of the Notified Body involved in the conformity assessment procedure

evidence.claim: Serious public health threat reports must be made immediately and not later than 2 calendar days after manufacturer awareness.
evidence.evidenceText: Serious public health threat: Immediately (without any delay that could not be justified) but not later than 2 calendar days after awareness by the Manufacturer of this threat.

evidence.claim: Death or unanticipated serious deterioration reports must be made immediately after link establishment and not later than 10 elapsed calendar days after awareness.
evidence.evidenceText: Death or Unanticipated serious deterioration in state of health: Immediately (without any delay that could not be justified) after the Manufacturer established a link between the device and the event but not later than 10 elapsed calendar days following the date of awareness of the event.

evidence.claim: Other incident reports must be made immediately after link establishment and not later than 30 elapsed calendar days after awareness.
evidence.evidenceText: Others: Immediately (without any delay that could not be justified) after the Manufacturer established a link between the device and the event but not later than 30 elapsed calendar days following the date of awareness of the event.

evidence.claim: After initial reporting, the manufacturer starts investigation and reports process information to the competent authority.
evidence.evidenceText: After initial report, Manufacturer starts to investigate generally and report to the competent authority how process.

evidence.claim: Investigation monitoring includes investigation action or method, process or schedule, and result.
evidence.evidenceText: The monitoring includes following provisions. - Investigation’s action or method - Process or schedule - Result

evidence.claim: If the manufacturer cannot perform the incident investigation, it should inform the Competent Authority without delay.
evidence.evidenceText: If the Manufacturer is not able to perform the investigation of an incident then he should inform the Competent Authority without delay.

evidence.claim: Possible vigilance outcomes include no action, surveillance, user information by advisory notice, corrective action, recall, and positive experience.
evidence.evidenceText: Outcomes may include, for example - No action - Additional surveillance or follow-up of devices in use - Dissemination of information to users, eg by advisory notice - Corrective action on future production - Corrective action on devices in use - Recall - Positive experience

evidence.claim: Manufacturers should issue advisory notices when implementing recalls.
evidence.evidenceText: The Manufacturer should issue advisory notices when implementing recalls.

evidence.claim: A device recall is removal from the field of a marketed device that violates related regulations.
evidence.evidenceText: The recall of a device is the removal from the field of a marketed device that is in violation of the related regulations.

evidence.claim: Recall excludes market withdrawal and stock recovery.
evidence.evidenceText: Recall does not include market withdrawal or stock recovery.

evidence.claim: A recall is necessary when there is a risk of serious deterioration of health or death.
evidence.evidenceText: A recall is necessary when it has been determined that there is a risk of serious deterioration of the state of health or death.

evidence.claim: A recall may include return, on-site modification, exchange, or destruction of a medical device.
evidence.evidenceText: A recall includes the return of a medical device to the supplier; its modification by the supplier at the site of installation; its exchange; or its destruction.

evidence.claim: An advisory notice provides information or advice on action for use, modification, disposal, or return of a medical device.
evidence.evidenceText: An advisory notice is issued to provide information and/or advice on what action should be taken in the use, modification, disposal or return of a medical device.

evidence.claim: The recall committee includes the President, Quality Assurance Manager, and Production Manager.
evidence.evidenceText: The committee includes the President, Quality Assurance Manager and Production Manager.

evidence.claim: In an emergency, the President or Quality Assurance Manager alone is authorized to initiate a recall.
evidence.evidenceText: In an emergency, and when there is no time to assemble the full committee, the President or the Quality Assurance Manager alone is authorized to initiate a recall.

evidence.claim: Field Safety Notices should be written in languages accepted by the National Competent Authority.
evidence.evidenceText: The FIELD SAFETY NOTICE should be written in the language(s) accepted by the National Competent Authority(s).

evidence.claim: Field Safety Notices should omit comments that inappropriately downplay risk or advertise products or services.
evidence.evidenceText: Any comments and descriptions that attempt to a) serve to play down the level of risk in an inappropriate manner, b) advertise products or services should be omitted.
